content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://www.globalcarbonproject.org/news/PermafrostCarbon.html", "source_title": "News: Expert assessment of vulnerability of permafrost carbon", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Climatic Change (2013) 119:359\u2013374 DOI 10.1007/s10584-013-0730-7 Lead The overall amount, rate, and form of C released to the atmosphere in ...", "direct_quote": "Climatic Change (2013) 119:359\u2013374 DOI 10.1007/s10584-013-0730-7 Lead The overall amount, rate, and form of C released to the atmosphere in a warmer world will influence the strength of the permafrost C feedback to climate change.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "For the highest warming scenario (RCP 8.5), experts hypothesized that C release from permafrost zone soils could be 19\u201345 Pg C by 2040, 162\u2013...", "direct_quote": "For the highest warming scenario (RCP 8.5), experts hypothesized that C release from permafrost zone soils could be 19\u201345 Pg C by 2040, 162\u2013288 Pg C by 2100, and 381\u2013616 Pg C by 2300 in CO2 equivalent using 100-year CH4 global warming potential (GWP)", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "These values become 50 % larger using 20-year CH4 GWP, with a third to a half of expected climate forcing coming from CH4 even though CH4 wa...", "direct_quote": "These values become 50 % larger using 20-year CH4 GWP, with a third to a half of expected climate forcing coming from CH4 even though CH4 was only 2.3 % of the expected C release.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
